description: §19.4.3 — a recovery arm's value lands in the assigned cell (g-handler-recovery-into-cell). `@result = risky(0) !{ ::NotFound(reason) :> "recovered: " + reason }` — risky(0) fails with NotFound("was-zero"); the recovery arm runs and its value MUST be written into @result. Clicking #go leaves @result = "recovered: was-zero" (NOT the raw {__scrml_error,...} envelope). Also exercises the single-field bind: reason = "was-zero" (the field value, not the whole .data object). Driven by a user click.
rationale: §19.4.3 — the `!{}` inline handler recovers a failed `!`-call: a non-escalating arm produces a value that becomes the result of the guarded expression. For a reactive assignment `@cell = call() !{...}`, that recovery value must be written into the cell — otherwise the raw error envelope leaks into @cell on the failure path. The recovered value "recovered: was-zero" (with reason bound to the NotFound field per §51.3.2) is the final cell value.
